betterquesting:10.desc:8:
The §6Launch Pad§f is the single-block foundation every AR rocket needs to take off. It defines the §6origin§f of the rocket and stores the launch coordinates that the §6Rocket Assembling Machine§f (10022) reads. Painted with §6black and yellow stripes§f by default, it must be placed on a flat surface (3×3 or larger) of solid blocks with a clear 3×3 column of air above for the rocket entity to spawn.

§6Production Path (launchpad.json)§f:
- Type: §6minecraft:crafting_shapeless§f
- Input: 1x §6Concrete Tile§f (advancedrocketry:concrete, quest 10000), 1x §6Black Dye§f (forge:ore_dict dyeBlack), 1x §6Yellow Dye§f (forge:ore_dict dyeYellow)
- Output: 1x §6Launch Pad§f (advancedrocketry:launchpad)

In the CT script `advancedRocketryTweaks.zs` the launch pad is not modified; the vanilla recipe is preserved. The launch pad has no GUI; right-clicking it does nothing. To use it, place a §6Rocket Assembling Machine§f (10022) adjacent and right-click the assembler to bind it to the pad.

The launch pad is the prerequisite for the §6Rocket Assembling Machine§f (10022), the §6Docking Pad§f (10023), and every rocket that flies afterwards. Without at least one launch pad, you cannot validate any rocket structure, no matter how complete.

§eTip§f: Place at least a 5×5 cleared area around the launch pad — the rocket needs 1 block of clearance on every side. If the §6Rocket Assembling Machine§f complains "Invalid Launchpad" the pad is not on the same Y level as the assembler or the air column is obstructed.
